steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a transitions question. The first sentence gives a 1965 milestone — the first syndicated comic strip by a Black cartoonist. The second sentence introduces two later strips from 1994 and 1996. The blank must signal that those strips came after the 1965 event, not before it or as a restatement of it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — That is,: Signals a restatement or clarification of the prior sentence. The second sentence introduces entirely new events (1994, 1996) rather than rephrasing the 1965 milestone, so this transition is illogical. ✗
  • Choice B — Previously,: Signals that something happened before a reference point. Because 1994 and 1996 come after 1965, using "previously" reverses the actual timeline. ✗
  • Choice C — For example,: Signals that a specific instance illustrates a general claim. The first sentence states a singular milestone, not a general claim that the later strips exemplify; the strips are follow-on events, not examples of the 1965 milestone. ✗
  • Choice D — Later,: Signals forward movement in time from 1965 to 1994 and 1996, which is exactly the relationship between the two sentences. ✓
That is the correct answer.
